negative_cases.why_wrong: Continuous Laplace Nd Filter: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Apply the numerator/denominator transfer function to the input waveform.

Continuous Laplace Nd Filter: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use laplace_nd() as a continuous-time behavioral transfer function fail.
Continuous Laplace Nd Filter: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Continuous Laplace Nd Filter: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Apply the numerator/denominator transfer function to the input waveform fails.

Continuous Laplace Nd Filter: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use laplace_nd() as a continuous-time behavioral transfer function fail.
